title: lecocarpus lecocarpoides subsp. brachyceratus
intro: Lecocarpus lecocarpoides subsp. brachyceratus, also known as the alpine dodonaea, is a plant species that belongs to the family Sapindaceae. This plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly its leaves, which are used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Lecocarpus lecocarpoides subsp. brachyceratus include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate pain and reduce the risk of infections. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or topical applications, although further research is needed to fully explore its potential uses and benefits.
